_comment: Canonical developer toolchain cache directories worth excluding from Defender realtime scans. Trades a small attack-surface increase for large I/O speedups on npm install / cargo build / pip install / docker build. Real-time protection stays on globally — only these subdirectories are added to Add-MpPreference -ExclusionPath. Fields: id, name, path (env vars expanded at runtime), languages (array), rationale, containsExecutables (bool — if true, adds real risk: Defender would normally scan node_modules/*.exe, esbuild binaries, .cargo/bin/*.exe), riskNote. The defender module snapshots the current Add-MpPreference list before adding, so revert is one-shot.
_scanPolicy: The apply script should verify each path EXISTS before excluding it (avoid noisy false paths). Also warn the user if any path resolves outside the user profile — that suggests a non-standard config we shouldn't blanket-exclude.

entries.name: npm cache (Roaming)
entries.rationale: npm download+tarball cache. Regenerable. Heavy churn during install.
entries.riskNote: Low risk. Tarballs are opened by npm, not directly executed.

entries.name: npm cache (Local)
entries.rationale: Alternate npm cache location on some setups.
entries.riskNote: Low risk.

entries.name: pnpm content-addressable store
entries.rationale: pnpm's global content-addressable store. Hard-linked into node_modules — Defender scans it repeatedly per project without exclusion.
entries.riskNote: Contains .exe binaries from packages like esbuild, sharp, prisma. Excluding means those aren't RT-scanned. Consider excluding only the package tarballs subdir (%LOCALAPPDATA%\pnpm-store\v3\files) if paranoid.

entries.name: Yarn cache
entries.rationale: Yarn tarball cache.
entries.riskNote: Low risk.

entries.name: pip cache
entries.rationale: pip wheel + http cache. High churn.
entries.riskNote: Low risk — wheels are unpacked by pip at install time, not directly executed.

entries.name: Python per-user site
entries.rationale: Per-user Python site-packages roots (e.g. Python313\site-packages).
entries.riskNote: Contains .pyd + .dll from wheels. Defender still scans installed .exe entry points elsewhere. Acceptable trade for build speed.

entries.name: uv cache
entries.rationale: Astral uv global cache. Very heavy write churn.
entries.riskNote: Low risk.

entries.name: Poetry cache
entries.rationale: Poetry download + virtualenv cache.
entries.riskNote: Low risk.

entries.name: Cargo home
entries.rationale: Cargo registry cache, git cache, and installed binaries. Cargo build is one of the most scan-sensitive workloads on Windows.
entries.riskNote: Contains %USERPROFILE%\.cargo\bin\*.exe (rustup, cargo, and cargo install targets). If concerned, exclude only %USERPROFILE%\.cargo\registry and %USERPROFILE%\.cargo\git.

entries.name: rustup toolchains
entries.rationale: rustup toolchain cache — rustc + std lib + linked binaries.
entries.riskNote: Contains toolchain .exe files (rustc, cargo, ld). These come from Rust's official signed distribution.

entries.name: Go module cache
entries.rationale: Go module cache (pkg/mod).
entries.riskNote: Low risk — module cache holds sources/zips.

entries.name: Go installed binaries
entries.rationale: Binaries installed via go install.
entries.riskNote: Contains user-built .exe files. Excluding means self-built Go tools skip RT-scan — acceptable for a dev's own builds.

entries.name: Gradle user home
entries.rationale: Gradle dependency cache, wrapper distributions, daemon working data.
entries.riskNote: Contains gradle wrapper distributions with launcher .exe. Signed by Gradle.

entries.name: Maven local repository
entries.rationale: Maven local artifact repo. Heavy churn during Maven builds.
entries.riskNote: Low risk — jars are read by JVM, not directly executed.

entries.name: NuGet global packages
entries.rationale: NuGet global-packages folder.
entries.riskNote: Contains .dll and analyzer .exe files. Signed NuGet packages primarily.

entries.name: NuGet HTTP cache
entries.rationale: NuGet HTTP response cache.
entries.riskNote: Low risk.

entries.name: Docker Desktop local state
entries.rationale: Docker Desktop local state + WSL2 disk images. Massive I/O during docker build / pull.
entries.riskNote: Contains WSL2 vhdx images and container filesystem layers with arbitrary executables. Excluding means Defender does NOT scan anything that runs inside containers via this path — but Defender can't reliably scan inside a Linux ext4 vhdx anyway, so this is honest.

entries.name: WSL Ubuntu ext4 vhdx
entries.rationale: WSL2 root disk image. Defender scans on every WSL syscall touching it without exclusion.
entries.riskNote: Windows-side Defender can't meaningfully scan inside an ext4 vhdx. Exclusion is more honest than pretending it protects the WSL guest.

entries.name: VS Code extensions
entries.rationale: VS Code extensions dir. Extension updates rewrite many small files.
entries.riskNote: Contains extension binaries. Marketplace-signed but not audited.

entries.name: Cursor AppData
entries.rationale: Cursor editor working data.
entries.riskNote: Same profile as VS Code — extensions + workspace cache.
